doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_dim (mpfr/src/dim.c L24-L47). Positive difference function: dim(b, c) = max(0, b - c). If b is NaN or c is NaN, returns NaN. If b > c, returns mpfr_sub(b, c, prec, rnd). Otherwise (b <= c, including the b == c case and the b < c case): returns +0 at the target prec, ternary 0. The C body uses mpfr_cmp to dispatch; TS uses the schema's compareMPFR (or inline comparison) to the same effect. NaN dispatch comes first; cmp_p of NaN-with-anything would itself throw, so we check NaN before cmp.
c_signature: int mpfr_dim(mpfr_ptr z, mpfr_srcptr x, mpfr_srcptr y, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode)
divergence_from_c: C takes (z, x, y, rnd) and mutates z. TS takes (b, c, prec, rnd): prec is explicit, returns Result {value, ternary}. C uses MPFR_SET_NAN+MPFR_RET_NAN for the NaN case (sets the side-channel ERANGE flag); TS returns NAN_VALUE with ternary 0. C uses mpfr_cmp directly — if one of x or y is NaN, mpfr_cmp internally sets erange and returns 0 (==), but the C dim doesn't reach that path because the NaN check is up front. TS mirrors the order: NaN dispatch first, then a comparison that knows both inputs are non-NaN. The b > c branch delegates to mpfr_sub (TS: mpfr_sub(b, c, prec, rnd)); the b <= c branch returns posZero(prec) with ternary 0 (exact).
refs: mpfr/src/dim.c L24-L47 — the C reference body.
src/ops/sub.ts — the load-bearing delegate for the b > c branch.
src/ops/cmp.ts — comparison primitive (or internal compareMPFR for the non-NaN compare).
src/core.ts — locked schema; posZero for the zero branch.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts': NaN ≠ NaN — handle NaN before any comparison.
